subtasks.title: Integrate Advanced Agent Capabilities and Implement Tooling Validation Script
subtasks.description: Configure advanced agent capabilities, including Context7, Exa, RTK-aware operating rules, bounded context budgets, code-review-graph, Serena, ast-grep, and Repomix, within the existing agent configuration and validate them through the direct-tool workflow.
subtasks.details:
Extend '.codex/config.toml' to integrate and configure the specified advanced agent capabilities, defining necessary hooks, operating rules, and context budgets for each. Keep the verification path on direct tool invocations such as `codex doctor`, pinned external Task Master CLI commands, `agent-skills-lint`, and the existing quality gates.

Durable control-plane results now folded into the repo:
- `npm run deps:tree` is a first-class dependency-tree integrity gate.
- Promptfoo, markdownlint-cli2, agent-skills-lint, and Task Master run as pinned external CLIs.
- The local provider eval lane is validated separately from the deterministic smoke lane.
- Task Master runtime policy now lives under `.taskmaster/`.
- The active orchestration posture is root plus direct specialists by default, with `agents.max_depth = 1` unless a later validator-backed experiment re-enables recursion.
- Task Master MCP uses the custom minimal surface `get_tasks,next_task,get_task,set_task_status,update_subtask,parse_prd`, while heavier graph analysis, regeneration, and mutation route through the pinned CLI.
- Current provider evidence shows the strongest committed local lane is `qwen2.5-coder:7b-instruct` for main/research with `qwen3:latest` as the local fallback, while remote providers remain explicit rescue lanes rather than the default runtime.
- `code-review-graph` and `semgrep` support verified `uvx` invocation paths, reducing persistent global-tool assumptions.
- Externalizing `task-master-ai` removed the vulnerable subtree from the repo-owned npm graph while preserving Task Master CLI and MCP behavior.

Current validation expectation:
- `codex doctor --summary --ascii --no-color`
- `npm run skills:lint`
- `npm run policy:check`
- `npm run verify:core`
- `npm run verify:all`
- `npx --yes --package task-master-ai@0.43.1 -c 'task-master validate-dependencies'`

tasks.title: Implement Core Library Domain Models
tasks.description: Define the Django models representing the core library domain, including works, editions, copies, loans, audit events, and external source records.
tasks.details: This task addresses Capability C2. Implement `BibliographicWork`, `Contributor`, `WorkContributor`, `BookEdition`, `BookCopy`, `Loan`, `AuditEvent`, and `ExternalSourceRecord` models within the `catalog`, `inventory`, `circulation`, and `audit` apps as per section 6.2. Implement fields and relationships as detailed in section 11.1 (Data Model). Crucially, apply the partial unique constraint to prevent more than one active loan per copy (NFR-006) and ensure valid ISBNs are normalized and invalid ones fail validation (C2.F2). Create initial migrations for these models.
tasks.testStrategy: Write unit tests for each model to verify field types, relationships, and custom validations (e.g., ISBN normalization, invalid ISBN rejection). Implement property-based tests (NFR-008) for loan invariants, ensuring that a copy cannot have more than one active loan and status transitions are correct. Run `uv run python manage.py makemigrations` and `uv run python manage.py migrate` to ensure migrations apply correctly.

subtasks.title: Implement Catalog Core Domain Models
subtasks.description: Define the `BibliographicWork`, `Contributor`, and `WorkContributor` Django models within the `catalog` application. These models will establish the foundational structure for managing bibliographic information.
subtasks.details:
Create or update `catalog/models.py` to include `BibliographicWork` (e.g., title, description, publication year), `Contributor` (e.g., name, role), and `WorkContributor` (to link works and contributors with specific roles). Ensure all fields and relationships align with section 11.1 (Data Model).
<info added on 2026-06-19T01:02:38.343Z>
Split `catalog/models.py` into `managers.py` and `querysets.py`, kept legacy reexports stable through `models.py`, and added boundary regression coverage in `tests/unit/catalog/test_models.py`. Validation: targeted pyright, targeted pytest, and `git diff --check` on touched files.
</info added on 2026-06-19T01:02:38.343Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Write unit tests for `BibliographicWork`, `Contributor`, and `WorkContributor` models to verify field types, relationships (e.g., ManyToMany through WorkContributor), and basic object creation.

subtasks.title: Implement BookEdition and BookCopy Models with ISBN Validation
subtasks.description: Define the `BookEdition` Django model within the `catalog` application and the `BookCopy` Django model within the `inventory` application, incorporating robust ISBN normalization and validation logic for the edition model.
subtasks.details: Create or update `catalog/models.py` to define `BookEdition` (e.g., ISBN, publisher, publication date, foreign key to `BibliographicWork`) and `inventory/models.py` to define `BookCopy` (e.g., acquisition date, status, foreign key to `BookEdition`). Implement custom validation for the ISBN field in `BookEdition` to normalize valid ISBNs (ISBN-10 to ISBN-13, remove hyphens) and reject invalid ones, as per requirement C2.F2. Consider a custom `ISBNField` or model `clean` method.
subtasks.testStrategy: Write unit tests for `BookEdition` and `BookCopy` models. Focus on ISBN validation: test valid ISBNs (10-digit, 13-digit, with/without hyphens) for normalization and invalid ISBNs for proper rejection. Verify relationships to `BibliographicWork` and other fields.

subtasks.title: Implement Circulation Loan Model with Partial Unique Constraint
subtasks.description: Define the `Loan` Django model within the `circulation` application, including a crucial partial unique constraint to prevent more than one active loan per copy (NFR-006).
subtasks.details: Create or update `circulation/models.py` to define the `Loan` model (e.g., `book_copy` Foreign Key, `borrower` Foreign Key, `loan_date`, `due_date`, `return_date`, `status` field). Implement a `UniqueConstraint` with a `condition` in the model's `Meta` class to enforce that only one `Loan` record can have an 'active' status for a given `BookCopy` at any time. The `status` field should be an Enum or choices field, with 'active' being one of the possible states.
subtasks.testStrategy: Write unit tests for the `Loan` model. Verify field types and relationships. Crucially, write tests that attempt to create multiple active loans for the same `BookCopy` and ensure the `UniqueConstraint` correctly raises a `ValidationError` or `IntegrityError`.

subtasks.title: Implement Audit Event and External Source Record Models
subtasks.description: Define the `AuditEvent` model within the `audit` application and the `ExternalSourceRecord` model within the `catalog` application.
subtasks.details: Create `audit/models.py` and define `AuditEvent` (e.g., `timestamp`, `user`, `action_type`, `related_object_id`, `related_object_content_type`, `details`). Create or update `catalog/models.py` to include `ExternalSourceRecord` (e.g., `source_name`, `source_id`, `url`, `last_synced_date`, Foreign Key to a `BibliographicWork` or `BookEdition`). Ensure fields and relationships align with section 11.1 (Data Model).
subtasks.testStrategy: Write unit tests for `AuditEvent` and `ExternalSourceRecord` models to verify field types, default values, and relationships. Ensure `AuditEvent` correctly handles generic foreign keys if implemented.

After defining all models, run `python manage.py makemigrations catalog inventory circulation audit` to generate the initial migration files. Review the generated migration files and their corresponding SQL output (`python manage.py sqlmigrate <app_name> <migration_id>`) to ensure they correctly reflect the model definitions, including primary keys, foreign keys, constraints (especially the partial unique constraint for Loan), and field types.
<info added on 2026-06-17T12:27:39.832Z>
Initial migrations for catalog, inventory, circulation, and audit applications have been verified to be correct using `uv run python manage.py makemigrations --check --dry-run`, which reported no changes detected. The model test suites for catalog and circulation also passed as part of the branch validation run, confirming that the initial migrations accurately reflect the implemented domain models.
</info added on 2026-06-17T12:27:39.832Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Apply the generated migrations to a clean test database (`python manage.py migrate`). Verify that all tables are created correctly and that the database schema matches the intended model definitions. Attempt to violate the partial unique constraint for the Loan model directly in the database to confirm it is enforced at the DB level.

tasks.title: Configure Authentication and Role-Based Access Control
tasks.description: Set up user authentication, define role-based access control (RBAC) using Django groups and permissions, and integrate `django-allauth` for optional OAuth readiness.
tasks.details: This task aligns with Capability C3. Use Django's built-in authentication system. Create `Admin`, `Librarian`, and `Member` groups and assign default permissions as per the RBAC matrix (section 17.1). Develop server-side authorization services (`accounts` module) that provide permission checks (e.g., `require_librarian`, `can_manage_catalog`) for views, APIs, and services (FR-011, NFR-003). Integrate `django-allauth` for OAuth-ready SSO, ensuring local demo can run with username/password and OAuth is environment-driven and optional (C3.F3).
tasks.testStrategy: Implement unit tests for authorization utilities (`can_manage_catalog`, `require_librarian`) to verify correct allow/deny decisions based on user roles and target objects. Write integration tests to confirm that Member direct POST attempts to mutate the catalog fail, and anonymous direct POSTs also fail (C3.F2, Scenario: Member cannot mutate catalog in 19.2). Verify that `django-allauth` can be configured via environment variables and does not prevent the app from running without them.

subtasks.title: Set up Django 'accounts' app and basic authentication
subtasks.description: Create the 'accounts' Django app to house authentication and authorization logic, register it, and confirm Django's default user authentication system is functioning correctly.
subtasks.details: Create `src/libraryops/accounts` app. Add 'accounts' to `INSTALLED_APPS` in `src/libraryops/config/settings/base.py`. Verify that Django's built-in authentication views (login/logout) can be accessed and function using the default User model.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run `python manage.py check`. Manually test basic user creation and login/logout functionality via Django admin or custom minimal views to ensure the default auth system is active.

subtasks.title: Define RBAC Groups and Default Permissions
subtasks.description: Create the 'Admin', 'Librarian', and 'Member' Django groups and assign their initial default permissions as specified in the RBAC matrix (section 17.1).
subtasks.details: Develop an initial data migration (or a custom management command in the `accounts` app) to create Django `Group` instances for 'Admin', 'Librarian', and 'Member'. Programmatically assign relevant permissions (e.g., 'can_add_book', 'can_view_loan') to these groups as per the defined RBAC matrix. Admin should have all permissions.
subtasks.testStrategy: Write unit tests to verify the existence of the created groups and that the correct permissions are assigned to each group. Run the migration/command and inspect groups/permissions via `python manage.py shell` or Django admin.

subtasks.title: Develop Server-Side Authorization Services
subtasks.description: Implement server-side authorization utility functions and decorators within the 'accounts' app, such as `require_librarian` and `can_manage_catalog`, to provide centralized permission checking.
subtasks.details: Within `src/libraryops/accounts/permissions.py` (or similar), create functions like `can_manage_catalog(user)` and decorators like `@require_librarian` that leverage Django's permission system (`user.has_perm()` or group checks). These services should provide permission checks for various operations on views, APIs, and services (FR-011, NFR-003).
subtasks.testStrategy: Implement unit tests for authorization utilities (e.g., `can_manage_catalog`, `require_librarian`) to verify correct allow/deny decisions based on mock user roles and permissions. Test edge cases with authenticated and unauthenticated users.

subtasks.title: Integrate and Configure django-allauth
subtasks.description: Install and configure `django-allauth` to enable robust authentication capabilities, including email verification and preparing for optional OAuth-based SSO.
subtasks.details:
Install `django-allauth`. Add required `allauth` apps to `INSTALLED_APPS` in `settings.py`. Configure `AUTHENTICATION_BACKENDS`, `LOGIN_REDIRECT_URL`, `LOGOUT_REDIRECT_URL`, and other necessary `allauth` settings. Include `allauth` URLs in `src/libraryops/config/urls.py`. Develop or override basic `allauth` templates for common flows (login, signup).
<info added on 2026-06-17T12:27:46.231Z>
Branch validation run confirmed `allauth` integration without regressions.
</info added on 2026-06-17T12:27:46.231Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify that `allauth` URLs are accessible. Manually test username/password registration and login flows through `allauth`'s default views. Ensure redirects work as configured for login/logout.

subtasks.title: Implement Environment-Driven OAuth and Local Demo Configuration
subtasks.description: Ensure `django-allauth` supports username/password login for local development and demo, while making OAuth providers environment-driven and optional for production readiness (C3.F3).
subtasks.details:
Confirm that `django-allauth` is configured to allow local demo with username/password login as the primary default. Implement logic in `settings.py` to conditionally enable/disable OAuth providers (e.g., Google, GitHub) based on the presence of specific environment variables (e.g., `SOCIAL_AUTH_GOOGLE_KEY`, `SOCIAL_AUTH_GOOGLE_SECRET`). This ensures OAuth is only active when explicitly configured in the environment.
<info added on 2026-06-17T12:27:57.951Z>
Ensure `django-allauth` is configured to allow local demo with username/password login as the primary default. Implement logic in `settings.py` to conditionally enable/disable OAuth providers (e.g., Google, GitHub) based on the presence of specific environment variables (e.g., `SOCIAL_AUTH_GOOGLE_KEY`, `SOCIAL_AUTH_GOOGLE_SECRET`). This ensures OAuth is only active when explicitly configured in the environment. Next steps include integrating OAuth provider toggles and completing the local demo configuration as per Task 5.
</info added on 2026-06-17T12:27:57.951Z>

Test with and without relevant environment variables set to ensure OAuth providers are conditionally enabled/disabled as expected. tasks.title: Implement Catalog Management UI and API
tasks.description: Develop user-facing catalog browse and detail pages, forms for creating/editing/archiving works, editions, and copies, cover image support, and configure Django Admin for internal management. Keep catalog management server-rendered through Django views, forms, selectors, and templates.
tasks.details: This task covers Capability C4. Create Django templates and views (`web` module) for catalog list and detail pages (C4.F1), showing availability and role-aware actions. Implement forms for `Admin`/`Librarian` to create, edit, and archive works, editions, and copies (C4.F2). Integrate cover image support, allowing external URLs or uploaded files with validation (C4.F3). Configure Django Admin (`admin.py` in `catalog`, `inventory` apps) for internal management, including list filters and search (C4.F4). Ensure audit events are recorded for all mutations (FR-001, FR-002, FR-003, NFR-010).
tasks.testStrategy: Write integration tests for catalog forms to ensure required fields are enforced and mutations result in persisted records and audit events. E2E tests (Scenario: Librarian creates a catalog record in 19.1) should verify the full workflow from UI. Confirm that archived records are hidden by default from public views. Test cover image upload validation (size, type). Verify Django Admin list views support search and permissions align with role policy.

subtasks.title: Implement Public Catalog Browse and Detail Pages
subtasks.description: Develop Django templates and views within the `web` module for listing works/editions and displaying detailed information, including availability and role-aware actions (C4.F1).

Create `catalog/views.py` functions and corresponding templates in `web/templates/catalog/` for `Work` list, `Edition` list, `Work` detail, and `Edition` detail. Use `catalog.selectors` to fetch data. Ensure availability status is displayed and actions are conditional on user roles (e.g., 'borrow' button for members, 'edit' for librarians).

tasks.title: Implement Circulation System
tasks.description: Implement the core checkout/borrow and checkin/return lifecycle services, transactional integrity, UI workflows for librarians/admins, and a loan dashboard.
tasks.details: This task addresses Capability C5. Develop `checkout_copy` and `return_copy` services within the `circulation` module, ensuring they are transactional (NFR-005) and enforce the 'only one active loan per copy' invariant (FR-006, NFR-006). Implement UI workflows (`web` module) for `Librarians`/`Admins` to perform checkout/return actions, potentially using HTMX modals (C5.F3). Create a loan dashboard (`web` module) to display active, overdue, and recent loans, with role-based visibility (C5.F4). Ensure audit events are recorded for all circulation actions (NFR-010).
tasks.testStrategy: Implement unit and property tests (NFR-008) for `checkout_copy` and `return_copy` services to verify copy status transitions, loan creation/completion, and enforcement of the active loan uniqueness constraint (FR-005, FR-006, FR-007, Scenario: Borrow in 19.3, Scenario: Return in 19.4). E2E tests should confirm that a `Librarian` can successfully checkout and return a copy via the UI. Verify role-based access to the loan dashboard (Members see only their own loans).

tasks.title: Implement Render-native Lexical Search
tasks.description: Implement exact-identifier-first lexical catalog search with PostgreSQL full-text ranking, faceted filtering, and deterministic result ordering.
tasks.details: This task covers Capability C6. Implement exact identifier search for ISBNs, barcodes, and external IDs (C6.F1). Configure PostgreSQL full-text search using weighted `tsvector` for keyword searches (C6.F2, section 12.2). Add filters/facets for availability, contributor, subject, language, and source (C6.F3). Implement deterministic ranking and human-readable explanations so exact identifiers outrank keyword matches and relevant lexical matches are understandable (C6.F4, section 12.3). No `SearchDocument` projection, embeddings, ParadeDB/BM25 adapter, or semantic/vector search is in scope for the current release.
tasks.testStrategy: Implement unit and integration tests for exact, FTS, and ranking behavior. Use search quality tests (section 12.4) with specific queries like ISBNs, author/title combinations, and natural language lexical queries to verify ranking policy. E2E tests should confirm that exact identifiers are prioritized and that facets still filter results correctly.

subtasks.title: Implement exact identifier search
subtasks.description: Create query helpers that prioritize ISBNs, barcodes, external IDs, and normalized title/contributor phrases.
subtasks.details: Implement the lexical search entrypoint and exact-match helpers in the search surface so exact identifiers route before all other search modes.
subtasks.testStrategy: Write unit tests to verify exact identifier search functionality and exact-match routing.

subtasks.title: Configure PostgreSQL full-text search
subtasks.description: Integrate PostgreSQL full-text search using weighted `tsvector` for keyword searches.
subtasks.details: Add the PostgreSQL search fields and safe query construction required for weighted lexical ranking. Keep the schema and indexing aligned with catalog/inventory data rather than a separate search-document projection.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run Django migrations and verify that the full-text index is created. Write integration tests for keyword search using specific titles and contributors.

subtasks.title: Implement faceted filtering
subtasks.description: Add filters for availability, contributor, subject, language, and source.
subtasks.details: Implement the faceted search inputs and query filtering logic using live catalog, inventory, and loan state.
subtasks.testStrategy: Write unit tests to ensure filters work as expected with various search queries.

subtasks.title: Implement deterministic ranking and explanations
subtasks.description: Merge exact and FTS results with deterministic ranking and readable result explanations.
subtasks.details: Make sure exact identifiers outrank keyword matches and that any boosts remain lexical and explainable.
subtasks.testStrategy: Write ranking tests that confirm exact identifiers outrank lexical matches and explanations remain stable.

tasks.title: Implement Reproducible Seed Data and Demo Corpus
tasks.description: Create app-owned Django management commands to seed demo roles, users, import a public-domain catalog, generate copies, and create example loan states for a reproducible demo dataset.
tasks.details:
This task covers Capability C8. Implement management commands as outlined in section 13.1, keeping the work in the owning app modules instead of a standalone `seed` package: `seed_roles`, `seed_demo_users` (with password reset, FR-012), `import_public_domain_catalog` (from Open Library/Project Gutenberg, FR-013), and `seed_circulation_examples`. Commands MUST be idempotent and support refresh/dry-run options. Seed Admin, Librarian, and Member demo accounts with documented credentials (C8.F1, section 13.2). Import curated public-domain records (up to 1,000) with provenance (C8.F2, FR-013). Generate realistic copies and example loans (C8.F3). Ensure imported corpora and downloaded dumps are not committed (section 13.3).
<info added on 2026-06-17T13:29:42Z>
Removed the empty seed package anchor, removed import-linter references, and reworded the PRD/spec planning surfaces to keep seed behavior in app-owned management-command workflows. Validated with import-linter, ast-grep, and import smoke checks.
</info added on 2026-06-17T13:29:42Z>
tasks.testStrategy: Run the seed/import commands with and without `--refresh` to verify idempotency and correct data generation. Confirm that demo users (admin@libraryops.demo, etc.) are created with specified roles and passwords. Verify that imported catalog records include source and license/copyright notes. Check that the loan dashboard displays active and overdue loans as expected. Use the 'Scenario: Demo seed can be refreshed' (19.6) to validate the seed process.

subtasks.details:

No code changes were required in this turn.
</info added on 2026-06-18T20:30:17.123Z>

Create users with specific passwords (e.g., 'admin@libraryops.demo', 'librarian@libraryops.demo') and assign them to the predefined roles created by `seed_roles`. Use Django's admin site for user management if needed.
<info added on 2026-06-17T17:51:01.674Z>

Checks: `uv run pyright src/libraryops/accounts/management/commands/seed_demo_users.py tests/integration/accounts/test_seed_commands.py` (0 errors), `uv run pytest -q tests/integration/accounts/test_seed_commands.py` (3 passed). subtasks.title: Create Django Management Command for `import_public_domain_catalog`
subtasks.description: Develop a management command to import curated public-domain catalog records, including provenance information.
subtasks.details:
Implement the `import_public_domain_catalog` command in `management/commands/import_public_domain_catalog.py`. Use Open Library/Project Gutenberg as the source and ensure that imported records include source links and copyright notes. Handle duplicates and provide a dry-run option to review changes without affecting the database.
<info added on 2026-06-19T11:15:35.516Z>
```
Implemented catalog-owned `import_public_domain_catalog` command with `--source`, `--limit`, `--dry-run`, and `--refresh`. Loader helpers are patchable for tests. Import writes works, editions, contributors, and `ExternalSourceRecord` provenance rows keyed by `(source_name, source_identifier)`; refresh reconciles the existing row in place; dry-run writes nothing. Validation/evidence: `scripts/codex-runtime-env.sh uv run pyright src/libraryops/catalog/management/commands/import_public_domain_catalog.py tests/integration/catalog/test_import_public_domain_catalog.py` (0 errors), `scripts/codex-runtime-env.sh uv run pytest -q tests/integration/catalog/test_import_public_domain_catalog.py` (4 passed).
```
</info added on 2026-06-19T11:15:35.516Z>

tasks.title: Prepare for Deployment and Generate Demo Evidence
tasks.description: Configure the Django application for live deployment, update evaluation-facing documentation, create a demo script, and tag a release to deliver a complete, reviewable product.
tasks.details: This task covers Capability C10 and C11. Configure Django app for deployment to Render or equivalent (C10.F1, section 24.6). Ensure production environment variables are managed securely and database migrations run. Update `README.md` (C10.F2) with the live URL, demo accounts, a feature checklist, setup instructions, and known limitations (section 19.3, 24.5). Prepare a Loom/demo script covering product, architecture, tests, and tradeoffs (C10.F3, section 16.2). Tag a SemVer release from `main` (C10.F3, section 16.4). Keep evaluator-facing documentation in the canonical README, runbook, and release surfaces rather than reviving deleted evidence-doc trees or a dedicated API surface.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify that the live URL loads, static assets are served, and database migrations apply successfully on the deployed environment. Confirm that demo accounts work on the live app. Review `README.md` to ensure all assignment criteria are mapped to visible evidence. Conduct a full run-through of the demo script to ensure it can be completed within the target time and accurately reflects the product's capabilities. Verify release tag corresponds to the deployed commit.

subtasks.title: Tag a Release
subtasks.description: Tag a SemVer release from `main` branch.

Create a Git tag with a version number following SemVer format (e.g., v1.2.3) and update the `CHANGELOG.md` file.

Durable release/governance state now folded into the repo:
- `CHANGELOG.md` exists and is managed through Python Semantic Release.
- `npm run release:check` verifies changelog/version readiness without publishing side effects.
- the GitHub release workflow is a read-only readiness workflow.
- Dependabot targets `development` and follows the documented integration path.
- workflow security remains pinned and validated through `actionlint` and `zizmor`.

tasks.title: Finalize Control-Plane Consolidation
tasks.description: Collapse remaining duplicate and stale control-plane documentation into the right source-of-truth surfaces, reconcile Task Master and Spec Kit artifacts to the implemented command/runtime model, and keep the repo in a coherent low-drift state while product implementation begins.
tasks.details: This task covers the remaining meta/control-plane work after Tasks 1 and 2. tasks.title: Implement Core Library Domain Models
tasks.description: Define the Django models representing the core library domain, including works, editions, copies, loans, audit events, and external source records.
tasks.details: This task addresses Capability C2. Implement `BibliographicWork`, `Contributor`, `WorkContributor`, `BookEdition`, `BookCopy`, `Loan`, `AuditEvent`, and `ExternalSourceRecord` models within the `catalog`, `inventory`, `circulation`, and `audit` apps as per section 6.2. Implement fields and relationships as detailed in section 11.1 (Data Model). Crucially, apply the partial unique constraint to prevent more than one active loan per copy (NFR-006) and ensure valid ISBNs are normalized and invalid ones fail validation (C2.F2). Create initial migrations for these models.
tasks.testStrategy: Write unit tests for each model to verify field types, relationships, and custom validations (e.g., ISBN normalization, invalid ISBN rejection). Implement property-based tests (NFR-008) for loan invariants, ensuring that a copy cannot have more than one active loan and status transitions are correct. Run `uv run python manage.py makemigrations` and `uv run python manage.py migrate` to ensure migrations apply correctly.

subtasks.title: Implement Catalog Core Domain Models

Create or update `catalog/models.py` to include `BibliographicWork` (e.g., title, description, publication year), `Contributor` (e.g., name, role), and `WorkContributor` (to link works and contributors with specific roles). Ensure all fields and relationships align with section 11.1 (Data Model).
<info added on 2026-06-19T01:02:38.343Z>
Split `catalog/models.py` into `managers.py` and `querysets.py`, kept legacy reexports stable through `models.py`, and added boundary regression coverage in `tests/unit/catalog/test_models.py`. Validation: targeted pyright, targeted pytest, and `git diff --check` on touched files.
</info added on 2026-06-19T01:02:38.343Z>

subtasks.title: Implement BookEdition and BookCopy Models with ISBN Validation
subtasks.description: Define the `BookEdition` Django model within the `catalog` application and the `BookCopy` Django model within the `inventory` application, incorporating robust ISBN normalization and validation logic for the edition model.
subtasks.details: Create or update `catalog/models.py` to define `BookEdition` (e.g., ISBN, publisher, publication date, foreign key to `BibliographicWork`) and `inventory/models.py` to define `BookCopy` (e.g., acquisition date, status, foreign key to `BookEdition`). Implement custom validation for the ISBN field in `BookEdition` to normalize valid ISBNs (ISBN-10 to ISBN-13, remove hyphens) and reject invalid ones, as per requirement C2.F2. Consider a custom `ISBNField` or model `clean` method.
subtasks.testStrategy: Write unit tests for `BookEdition` and `BookCopy` models. Focus on ISBN validation: test valid ISBNs (10-digit, 13-digit, with/without hyphens) for normalization and invalid ISBNs for proper rejection. Verify relationships to `BibliographicWork` and other fields.

subtasks.title: Implement Circulation Loan Model with Partial Unique Constraint
subtasks.description: Define the `Loan` Django model within the `circulation` application, including a crucial partial unique constraint to prevent more than one active loan per copy (NFR-006).
subtasks.details: Create or update `circulation/models.py` to define the `Loan` model (e.g., `book_copy` Foreign Key, `borrower` Foreign Key, `loan_date`, `due_date`, `return_date`, `status` field). Implement a `UniqueConstraint` with a `condition` in the model's `Meta` class to enforce that only one `Loan` record can have an 'active' status for a given `BookCopy` at any time. The `status` field should be an Enum or choices field, with 'active' being one of the possible states.
subtasks.testStrategy: Write unit tests for the `Loan` model. Verify field types and relationships. Crucially, write tests that attempt to create multiple active loans for the same `BookCopy` and ensure the `UniqueConstraint` correctly raises a `ValidationError` or `IntegrityError`.

subtasks.title: Implement Audit Event and External Source Record Models

Create or update `catalog/models.py` to include `ExternalSourceRecord` (e.g., `source_name`, `source_id`, `url`, `last_synced_date`, Foreign Key to a `BibliographicWork` or `BookEdition`). Ensure fields and relationships align with section 11.1 (Data Model).
subtasks.testStrategy: Write unit tests for `AuditEvent` and `ExternalSourceRecord` models to verify field types, default values, and relationships. Ensure `AuditEvent` correctly handles generic foreign keys if implemented.

tasks.title: Configure Authentication and Role-Based Access Control
tasks.description: Set up user authentication, define role-based access control (RBAC) using Django groups and permissions, and integrate `django-allauth` for optional OAuth readiness.
tasks.details: This task aligns with Capability C3. Use Django's built-in authentication system. Create `Admin`, `Librarian`, and `Member` groups and assign default permissions as per the RBAC matrix (section 17.1). Develop server-side authorization services (`accounts` module) that provide permission checks (e.g., `require_librarian`, `can_manage_catalog`) for views, APIs, and services (FR-011, NFR-003). Integrate `django-allauth` for OAuth-ready SSO, ensuring local demo can run with username/password and OAuth is environment-driven and optional (C3.F3).
tasks.testStrategy: Implement unit tests for authorization utilities (`can_manage_catalog`, `require_librarian`) to verify correct allow/deny decisions based on user roles and target objects. Write integration tests to confirm that Member direct POST attempts to mutate the catalog fail, and anonymous direct POSTs also fail (C3.F2, Scenario: Member cannot mutate catalog in 19.2). Verify that `django-allauth` can be configured via environment variables and does not prevent the app from running without them.

subtasks.title: Set up Django 'accounts' app and basic authentication
subtasks.description: Create the 'accounts' Django app to house authentication and authorization logic, register it, and confirm Django's default user authentication system is functioning correctly.
subtasks.details: Create `src/libraryops/accounts` app. Add 'accounts' to `INSTALLED_APPS` in `src/libraryops/config/settings/base.py`. Verify that Django's built-in authentication views (login/logout) can be accessed and function using the default User model.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run `python manage.py check`. Manually test basic user creation and login/logout functionality via Django admin or custom minimal views to ensure the default auth system is active.

Programmatically assign relevant permissions (e.g., 'can_add_book', 'can_view_loan') to these groups as per the defined RBAC matrix. Admin should have all permissions.

subtasks.title: Develop Server-Side Authorization Services
subtasks.description: Implement server-side authorization utility functions and decorators within the 'accounts' app, such as `require_librarian` and `can_manage_catalog`, to provide centralized permission checking.
subtasks.details: Within `src/libraryops/accounts/permissions.py` (or similar), create functions like `can_manage_catalog(user)` and decorators like `@require_librarian` that leverage Django's permission system (`user.has_perm()` or group checks). These services should provide permission checks for various operations on views, APIs, and services (FR-011, NFR-003).
subtasks.testStrategy: Implement unit tests for authorization utilities (e.g., `can_manage_catalog`, `require_librarian`) to verify correct allow/deny decisions based on mock user roles and permissions. Test edge cases with authenticated and unauthenticated users.

Configure `AUTHENTICATION_BACKENDS`, `LOGIN_REDIRECT_URL`, `LOGOUT_REDIRECT_URL`, and other necessary `allauth` settings. Include `allauth` URLs in `src/libraryops/config/urls.py`. Develop or override basic `allauth` templates for common flows (login, signup).

This ensures OAuth is only active when explicitly configured in the environment.

tasks.title: Implement Circulation System
tasks.description: Implement the core checkout/borrow and checkin/return lifecycle services, transactional integrity, UI workflows for librarians/admins, and a loan dashboard.
tasks.details: This task addresses Capability C5. Develop `checkout_copy` and `return_copy` services within the `circulation` module, ensuring they are transactional (NFR-005) and enforce the 'only one active loan per copy' invariant (FR-006, NFR-006). Implement UI workflows (`web` module) for `Librarians`/`Admins` to perform checkout/return actions, potentially using HTMX modals (C5.F3). Create a loan dashboard (`web` module) to display active, overdue, and recent loans, with role-based visibility (C5.F4). Ensure audit events are recorded for all circulation actions (NFR-010).
tasks.testStrategy: Implement unit and property tests (NFR-008) for `checkout_copy` and `return_copy` services to verify copy status transitions, loan creation/completion, and enforcement of the active loan uniqueness constraint (FR-005, FR-006, FR-007, Scenario: Borrow in 19.3, Scenario: Return in 19.4). E2E tests should confirm that a `Librarian` can successfully checkout and return a copy via the UI. Verify role-based access to the loan dashboard (Members see only their own loans).

tasks.title: Implement Render-native Lexical Search
tasks.description: Implement exact-identifier-first lexical catalog search with PostgreSQL full-text ranking, faceted filtering, and deterministic result ordering.
tasks.details: This task covers Capability C6. Implement exact identifier search for ISBNs, barcodes, and external IDs (C6.F1). Configure PostgreSQL full-text search using weighted `tsvector` for keyword searches (C6.F2, section 12.2). Add filters/facets for availability, contributor, subject, language, and source (C6.F3). Implement deterministic ranking and human-readable explanations so exact identifiers outrank keyword matches and relevant lexical matches are understandable (C6.F4, section 12.3). No `SearchDocument` projection, embeddings, ParadeDB/BM25 adapter, or semantic/vector search is in scope for the current release.
tasks.testStrategy: Implement unit and integration tests for exact, FTS, and ranking behavior. Use search quality tests (section 12.4) with specific queries like ISBNs, author/title combinations, and natural language lexical queries to verify ranking policy. E2E tests should confirm that exact identifiers are prioritized and that facets still filter results correctly.

tasks.title: Implement Reproducible Seed Data and Demo Corpus
tasks.description: Create app-owned Django management commands to seed demo roles, users, import a public-domain catalog, generate copies, and create example loan states for a reproducible demo dataset.
tasks.details:
This task covers Capability C8. Implement management commands as outlined in section 13.1, keeping the work in the owning app modules instead of a standalone `seed` package: `seed_roles`, `seed_demo_users` (with password reset, FR-012), `import_public_domain_catalog` (from Open Library/Project Gutenberg, FR-013), and `seed_circulation_examples`. Commands MUST be idempotent and support refresh/dry-run options. Seed Admin, Librarian, and Member demo accounts with documented credentials (C8.F1, section 13.2). Import curated public-domain records (up to 1,000) with provenance (C8.F2, FR-013). Generate realistic copies and example loans (C8.F3). Ensure imported corpora and downloaded dumps are not committed (section 13.3).
<info added on 2026-06-17T13:29:42Z>
Removed the empty seed package anchor, removed import-linter references, and reworded the PRD/spec planning surfaces to keep seed behavior in app-owned management-command workflows. Validated with import-linter, ast-grep, and import smoke checks.
</info added on 2026-06-17T13:29:42Z>

Update `README.md` (C10.F2) with the live URL, demo accounts, a feature checklist, setup instructions, and known limitations (section 19.3, 24.5). Keep evaluator-facing documentation in the canonical README, runbook, and release surfaces rather than reviving deleted evidence-doc trees or a dedicated API surface.

subtasks.title: Tag a Release
subtasks.description: Tag a SemVer release from `main` branch.
subtasks.details:
Create a Git tag with a version number following SemVer format (e.g., v1.2.3) and update the `CHANGELOG.md` file.

Durable release/governance state now folded into the repo:
- `CHANGELOG.md` exists and is managed through Python Semantic Release.
- `npm run release:check` verifies changelog/version readiness without publishing side effects.
- the GitHub release workflow is a read-only readiness workflow.
- Dependabot targets `development` and follows the documented integration path.
